response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and distributed systems engineer with strong software craftsmanship credentials, but he is a poor fit for this role. The minimum requirement of 8 years of C++ experience is the most critical dealbreaker — his resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments and a mention of C++ in a student thesis project, with the rest of his career almost entirely in TypeScript, JavaScript, DLang, and Node.js. He also lacks the cloud compute platform depth, formal technical leadership at scale, and systems-level infrastructure ownership the role demands.
strengths: Strong distributed systems exposure — worked on micro-services infrastructure handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Demonstrated software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code katas, and public teaching/mentoring through workshops and talks
Broad cross-stack experience including some cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) and DevOps infrastructure (GitLab, Docker, CI servers)
concerns: Critically insufficient C++ experience — roughly 6 months versus the 8-year minimum requirement; primary languages are TypeScript and JavaScript
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure project; cloud usage is incidental (AWS CDK for pricing, Kubernetes for a side project)
No formal technical leadership title or documented experience managing technical strategy, mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers, or driving cross-team alignment at scale
